summary: Plan-mode PR inventory classification for 40 candidate PRs. The job says this is not a dedupe cluster and provides no shared canonical. Validator repair routes the nine security-sensitive PR targets through central security handling with non-mutating route_security actions, while the remaining PRs stay conservatively independent for normal maintainer review.

actions.reason: Independent open PR with its own issue/fix scope; no closure, merge, or fix action is allowed in this plan shard.
Independent maintainer-review candidate; not a duplicate or superseded item in this shard.
Provider-specific compatibility PR with unique scope; keep for normal review rather than dedupe or close.
Security-boundary-labeled PR must be routed to central OpenClaw security handling and not classified through ProjectClownfish backlog cleanup.
Independent Plugin SDK feature work; failing checks make it review work, not closeout work.
Security-boundary-labeled CLI/tool execution PR must be routed to central OpenClaw security handling.
Independent compatibility feature PR; no duplicate or superseding candidate is hydrated.
No hydrated live state is available for a mutating decision; keep independent from this inventory shard.
Security-boundary-labeled skill setup execution PR must be routed to central OpenClaw security handling.
Independent message-delivery fix; no shared canonical or duplicate relationship is established.
Broad independent feature/fix candidate with validation failures; keep for maintainer review rather than close.
No hydrated live state is available for mutation; classify as independent documentation work.
Independent maintainer PR with no hydrated duplicate or closure evidence.
Independent bug-fix PR; no hydrated live state supports closure or merge planning.
Independent small PR; without hydrated live state and low-signal close permission, keep it for maintainer review.
Potential sensitive path disclosure must be quarantined with route_security instead of ordinary PR inventory classification.
Independent Canvas fix candidate; keep non-mutating due missing hydrated state.
Exec environment and secret-sanitization PR is security-sensitive and must be routed to central OpenClaw security handling.
Independent UI/i18n PR; keep for maintainer review.
Independent stabilization PR; no hydrated closure evidence.
Independent session-state/availability PR; keep non-mutating.
Plugin code-safety scanning and security-boundary-labeled PR must be routed to central OpenClaw security handling.
Independent performance PR; no hydrated duplicate or closure target.
Maintainer-owned independent PR with unresolved proof/review gates; keep out of dedupe closeout.
Independent sessions cleanup PR; no duplicate relationship is established.
Independent command/config migration PR; keep non-mutating due missing hydrated live state.
Independent memory-core bug fix; no shared canonical or closure path in this inventory shard.
Independent plugin-runtime PR; no hydrated closure evidence.
External-plugin and security-boundary-labeled PR must be routed to central OpenClaw security handling.
Independent provider compatibility PR with validation failures; keep for review/proof.
Independent SDK contract change with validation failures; no closeout path is justified.
Independent channel fix awaiting normal review/author path.
Security-boundary-labeled auto-reply/reset behavior PR must be routed to central OpenClaw security handling.
Independent channel feature PR; keep for maintainer review.
Independent provider auth fix; keep non-mutating due missing hydrated live state.
Independent webhooks fix with unresolved validation/review gates; no closure action is appropriate.
Independent export/rendering fix; keep for its own review path.
Independent message-delivery fix; dirty branch blocks merge but not classification.
Independent large delivery/session-state fix; pending/dirty gates block merge, not inventory classification.
Session key routing documentation flagged as security-sensitive must be quarantined with route_security rather than ordinary inventory classification.
